mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >60% of instances following the trigger, or the signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive forward returns during tightening periods, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.0% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, eroding real corporate margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.0% consistently precedes positive 60-day returns, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates aggregate demand resilience, reducing recession probability and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a liquidity-constrained regime signals supply chain bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, prompting immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns when credit spreads widen, or the signal shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.
